steps.prompt: Which word is the main verb?

hints.edo, edere, edi, esum: These are the parts of 'edere' (to eat) — not 'esse'
hints.eo, ire, ivi, itum: These are the parts of 'ire' (to go) — not 'esse'
hints.scio, scire, scivi, scitum: These are the parts of 'scire' (to know) — not 'esse'

hints.Ablative: Ablative would be 'historia' (same form but different function) — here it is clearly the subject
hints.Vocative: Cicero is not addressing history — it is the subject of 'est'

hints.2nd: 2nd declension nouns end in -us/-um — 'historia, historiae' is 1st declension
hints.3rd: 3rd declension nouns have varied endings — 'historia' with -a ending is 1st declension
hints.5th: 5th declension nouns end in -es — 'historia' is 1st declension

hints.Genitive: Genitive singular would also be 'testis' in form, but here it is a predicate nominative after 'est'
hints.Accusative: Accusative singular would be 'testem' — 'testis' here is nominative
hints.Ablative: Ablative would be 'teste' — 'testis' is the predicate nominative

hints.1st: 1st declension nouns end in -a — 'testis, testis' is 3rd declension
hints.2nd: 2nd declension nouns end in -us/-um — 'testis' is 3rd declension
hints.4th: 4th declension nouns end in -us/-u — 'testis' is 3rd declension

hints.Nominative: Nominative plural would be 'tempora' — 'temporum' is genitive plural
hints.Dative: Dative plural would be 'temporibus' — 'temporum' is genitive
hints.Accusative: Accusative plural would be 'tempora' — 'temporum' is genitive plural

hints.1st: 1st declension genitive plural ends in -arum — 'tempus, temporis' is 3rd declension
hints.2nd: 2nd declension genitive plural ends in -orum — 'temporum' is 3rd declension (neuter)
hints.4th: 4th declension genitive plural ends in -uum — 'temporum' is 3rd declension

hints.Accusative: Accusative would be 'lucem' — 'lux' is a second predicate nominative

hints.Nominative: Nominative would be 'veritas' — 'veritatis' is the genitive singular

hints.1st: 1st declension genitive ends in -ae — 'veritas, veritatis' is 3rd declension
hints.2nd: 2nd declension genitive ends in -i — 'veritatis' is 3rd declension
hints.5th: 5th declension genitive ends in -ei — 'veritatis' is 3rd declension

translation: History is the witness of the times, the light of truth.
